ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_code" TEXT /* Code of the station (e.g., CAN, TLY) */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the station (e.g., KANNUR, THALASSERY) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station (e.g., KANNUR, THALASSERY, India) */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the station */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the station for geographical mapping */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the station for geographical mapping */,
  "operational_hours" TEXT /* Operational hours of the station */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact number for the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trains" (
  "train_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each train */,
  "train_number" TEXT /* Number of the train (e.g., 12345) */,
  "train_name" TEXT /* Name of the train (e.g., Express, Local) */,
  "route_start" TEXT /* Starting station of the train route */,
  "route_end" TEXT /* Ending station of the train route */,
  "train_type" TEXT /* Type of the train (e.g., Express, Local, Superfast) */,
  "train_length" TEXT /* Length of the train in meters */,
  "average_speed" TEXT /* Average speed of the train in km/h */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of passengers the train can carry */,
  "last_service_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance or service check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("train_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule entry */,
  "train_id" INTEGER /* ID of the train */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station */,
  "arrives" TEXT /* Arrival time of the train at the station */,
  "halt_time" TEXT /* Duration of the halt at the station */,
  "departs" TEXT /* Departure time of the train from the station */,
  "platform" INTEGER /* Platform number where the train halts */,
  "locomotive_reverse" TEXT /* Whether the locomotive is reversed at this station (Yes/No) */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week the schedule applies (e.g., Monday, Tuesday) */,
  "is_weekend" TEXT /* Indicates if the schedule is for a weekend (Yes/No) */,
  "is_holiday" TEXT /* Indicates if the schedule is for a holiday (Yes/No) */,
  "estimated_arrival_time" TEXT /* Estimated arrival time based on predictive analytics */,
  "estimated_departure_time" TEXT /* Estimated departure time based on predictive analytics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schedules_train_id FOREIGN KEY ("train_id") REFERENCES trains ("train_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schedules_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station */,
  "platform_number" INTEGER /* Number of the platform */,
  "platform_type" TEXT /* Type of the platform (e.g., Main, Side) */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the platform in terms of number of trains it can handle simultaneously */,
  "is_disabled_access" TEXT /* Indicates if the platform has access for disabled passengers (Yes/No) */,
  "is_pins" TEXT /* Indicates if the platform has Passenger Information Systems (Yes/No) */,
  "last_inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the last safety inspection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_platforms_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locomotives" (
  "locomotive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each locomotive */,
  "locomotive_number" TEXT /* Number of the locomotive (e.g., L12345) */,
  "locomotive_type" TEXT /* Type of the locomotive (e.g., Diesel, Electric) */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the locomotive in terms of number of cars it can pull */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "fuel_type" TEXT /* Type of fuel used by the locomotive (e.g., diesel, electric) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the locomotive manufacturer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("locomotive_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "train_locomotive_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "train_id" INTEGER /* ID of the train */,
  "locomotive_id" INTEGER /* ID of the locomotive */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the assignment starts */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the assignment ends */,
  "maintenance_due" TEXT /* Indicates if the locomotive is due for maintenance (Yes/No) */,
  "next_assigned_train" INTEGER /* ID of the next train the locomotive will be assigned to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_train_locomotive_assignments_locomotive_id FOREIGN KEY ("locomotive_id") REFERENCES locomotives ("locomotive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_train_locomotive_assignments_train_id FOREIGN KEY ("train_id") REFERENCES trains ("train_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., station manager, train operator, passenger) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "is_active" TEXT /* Indicates if the user account is active (Yes/No) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the schedule being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the schedule */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the schedule was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_schedule_id FOREIGN KEY ("schedule_id") REFERENCES schedules ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "delays" (
  "delay_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each delay entry */,
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the schedule */,
  "delay_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the delay (e.g., technical issues, weather conditions) */,
  "delay_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the delay in minutes */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Person who reported the delay */,
  "reported_on" TEXT /* Date when the delay was reported */,
  "is_resolved" TEXT /* Indicates if the delay has been resolved (Yes/No) */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time it took to resolve the delay in minutes */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the resolution of the delay */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("delay_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_delays_schedule_id FOREIGN KEY ("schedule_id") REFERENCES schedules ("schedule_id")
)
